The HRV variability (58-85ms range) appears to correlate more with sleep quality than orthostatic stress, suggesting good autonomic reserve.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Temperature deviation averaging -0.1°C suggests no acute Hashimoto's flare activity currently. However, the consistently insufficient deep sleep (below 1.5h most nights) may be compromising immune system repair cycles needed to manage autoimmune activity.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: High readiness scores (80-91 range) and stable HRV indicate good capacity for vestibular rehabilitation most days. Only March 18th with lower readiness (80) and reduced sleep might warrant scaling back intensive balance training.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Prioritize deep sleep optimization by maintaining consistent 8-hour sleep windows and investigating sleep architecture - consider earlier magnesium glycinate timing or bedroom temperature adjustment to increase deep sleep from current 1.1h average to target 1.5-2h for better Hashimoto's management
